nuclear_envelope.notes: The Phase 2 render shows a double envelope and lumen, but the reference image has stronger anatomical section readability. The next visual pass should make the cutaway/layer relationship clearer from the wide view.

nuclear_pores.notes: Pores are raised and have gate cores, which avoids the open-hole misconception. However, the pore distribution and repeated subunit language should be more reference-driven and less sparse.

nuclear_lamina.notes: The lamina is still a hint, not a convincing gold support lattice. It needs stronger interior placement and more visible mesh structure before passing the lamina baseline.

organic_3d_translation.notes: The model has organic surface treatment, but the validation board shows the wide render still needs clearer premium infographic composition and more reference-aligned biological detail.

major_failures: NuclearLamina_Lattice is not visually developed enough compared to the lamina reference.
Wide render does not yet communicate the envelope/lumen/pore system as clearly as the reference baseline.
minor_issues: Perinuclear lumen glow may be too dependent on Blender material behavior.
Pore density is low for a final hero nucleus.
The Phase 2 board includes nucleoplasm as future reference only; nucleoplasm should not be built until envelope system improves.
required_fixes: Improve the envelope-system visual design before adding new nucleus internals.
Create a stronger lamina lattice pass inside the envelope.
Increase pore distribution quality while staying within Roblox budget and LOD rules.
Add a clearer cutaway or inspection angle that proves the outer membrane, lumen, inner membrane, pore, gate, and lamina relationships.
next_recommended_step: Run a focused Phase 3 envelope-system refinement: double envelope, lumen, pores, gate cores, and lamina only.
